name: collektions

metadata.name: collektions
metadata.summary: A collection of Python functions on iterables and collections inspired by the Kotlin collections API.

documentation:
# colleKtions
A collection of Python functions on iterables and collections inspired by the 
[Kotlin collections standard library API](https://kotlinlang.org/api/latest/jvm/stdlib/kotlin.collections/#functions).

For the curious, the name is a reference to the Kotlin collections API in two ways: 
1. The name collections, which is obvious
2. The intentional misspelling with a K since `kt` is the file extension used for Kotlin source 
   files.

variables.name: collektions._types.T
documentation: Typically used with iterables as the Type for each item in the iterable.

variables.name: collektions._types.R
documentation: Typically used as a Return or Result type usually from a transformation of some kind.

variables.name: collektions._types.K
documentation: Typically used with mappings as the Key type.

variables.name: collektions._types.V
documentation: Typically used with mappings as the Value type.

modules.name: collektions.preconditions
documentation:
Useful precondition functions modeled after Preconditions.kt.

Implementation-wise, these functions do similar things (minute differences in
which type of Exception is raised where), but they are meant to indicate different
things to a reader.

For example:
: - Use `require{,_not_none}` to validate function arguments before
    proceeding with the rest of the function's implementation.
  - Use `check{,_not_none}` to test assumptions about an object's state.

functions.name: collektions.preconditions.check
documentation:
Check that `condition` is `True`, and raise an exception of type `exc_type` if not.

By default, `exc_type` is a generic {py:obj}`RuntimeError`.

functions.name: collektions.preconditions.check_not_none
documentation: Check that `value` is not `None` and raise a `RuntimeError` if it is.

functions.name: collektions.preconditions.require
documentation:
Check that the requirement represented by `condition` is met.

Raises an exception of type `exc_type` if the requirement is not met.

By default, `exc_type` is {py:obj}`ValueError`.

functions.name: collektions.preconditions.require_not_none
documentation: Check that `value` is not `None` and raise a `ValueError` if it is.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.drop_last
documentation: Drop the last `number` items from `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.drop_last_while
documentation:
Drop items from `sequence` matching `predicate` from right to left.

In other words, all items at the end of `sequence` matching `predicate`
are dropped.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.fold_right
documentation:
Accumulates value starting from `initial_value`.

Accumulation starts from `initial_value` and applies `accumulator` from right
to left across `iterable` passing the current accumulated value with each item.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.fold_right_indexed
documentation:
Accumulates value starting from `initial_value`.

Accumulation starts from `initial_value` and applies `accumulator` from right
to left across `iterable` passing the current accumulated value, the current index,
and the curren item at that index.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.last
documentation:
Return the last item of `sequence` matching `predicate` or raise if no item matches.

Raises:

: ValueError: If no item matches `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.last_or_none
documentation:
Return the last item of `sequence` matching `predicate` or `None` if no item matches.

Returns:

: The last item from `sequence` that matches `predicate` or `None` if no item matches.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.take_last
documentation:
Return the last `n` items from `sequence`.

In almost all cases, the input sequence type is preserved in the return value.

In other words, if a string is the input sequence, a string will be the output. If a
range is the input sequence, then a range will be the output, etc.

functions.name: collektions._sequence.take_last_while
documentation:
Return the right-most items in `sequence` satisfying `predicate`.

In other words, all items in `sequence` working backwards from the end satisfying
`predicate` are returned. As soon as the first item not matching predicate is found
iteration stops and no more items to the left of the first non-matching item are evaluated.

modules.name: collektions.shadow
documentation:
Override some built-in functions in order to provide a consistent interface.

In other words, these functions hide the built-in functions of the same name so
that calling code can have a consistent interface across higher-order function
use in their code.

For example, rather than calling code having

> head = first_or_none(iterable, predicate)
> tail = last_or_none(iterable, predicate)
> flat = flat_map(iterable1, iterable2, ..., mapping)
> [sum] = sum_by(iterable, selector)

mixed in with built-ins of a different interface like

> transformed = map(mapping, iterable)
> at_least_one = any(iterable_of_bools)
> all_of_them = all(iterable_of_bools)

shadows like `map`, `any`, and `all` can be imported explicitly from this
module to instead have consistent interfaces across their higher-order collections
functions:

> head = first_or_none(iterable, predicate)
> tail = last_or_none(iterable, predicate)
> flat = flat_map(iterable1, iterable2, ..., mapping)
> [sum] = sum_by(iterable, selector)
> transformed = map(iterable, mapping)
> at_least_one = any(iterable, predicate)
> all_of_them = all(iterable, predicate)

functions.name: collektions.shadow.all
documentation: Overrides built-in {py:func}`all` to provide an interface consistent with this library.

functions.name: collektions.shadow.any
documentation: Overrides built-in {py:func}`any` to provide an interface consistent with this library.

functions.name: collektions.shadow.filter
documentation: Overrides built-in {py:func}`filter` to provide an interface consistent with this library.

functions.name: collektions.shadow.map
documentation: Overrides built-in {py:func}`map` to provide an interface consistent with this library.

functions.name: collektions._mapping.filter_keys
documentation: Return a new mapping of all key/value pairs from `mapping` where `key` satisfies `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._mapping.filter_values
documentation: Return a new mapping of all key/value pairs from `mapping` where `value` satisfies `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._mapping.map_keys
documentation:
Return a new mapping with a key set formed by applying `transform` to the original key set.

The value set in the new mapping is the same as the value set in the original mapping.

functions.name: collektions._mapping.map_keys_to
documentation:
Update `destination` with `mapping`.

Entries from the original `mapping` have their keys transformed by applying `transform`,
and their values unchanged.

functions.name: collektions._mapping.map_values
documentation:
Return a new mapping w/ a value set formed by applying `transform` to the original value set.

The key set in the new mapping is the same as the key set in the original mapping.

functions.name: collektions._mapping.map_values_to
documentation:
Update `destination` with `mapping`.

Entries from the original `mapping` have their keys unchanged, and their values transformed
by applying `transform`.

modules.name: collektions._iterable
documentation: Functional-esque tools for Python inspired by the Kotlin Collections API.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.associate
documentation: Transform `iterable` in to a mapping of key, value pairs as returned by `transform`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.associate_to
documentation: Update `destination` with new entries from `iterable` transformed by `transform`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.associate_by
documentation:
Map items in `iterable` by keys prescribed by `key_transform`.

Put another way, turn `iterable` in to a mapping of key, value pairs where the keys
are prescribed by `key_transform` and the values are the original items in `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.associate_by_to
documentation:
Update `destination` with new entries from `iterable`.

The keys in the new entries are prescribed by `key_transform`, and the values are the
original items in `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.associate_with
documentation:
Map items in `iterable` to values prescribed by `value_transform`.

Put another way, turn `iterable` in to a mapping of key, value pairs where the keys
are the original items in `iterable` and the values are prescribed by `value_transform`.

The items in `iterable` must be hashable.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.associate_with_to
documentation:
Update `destination` with new entries from `iterable`.

The keys in the new entries are the original items in `iterable` and the values
are prescribed by `value_transform`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.average
documentation:
Return the average (mean) of the values in `iterable`.

If `iterable` has no items, then this function returns `float("NaN")`,
which can be checked by `math.isnan(average(...))`.

All items in `iterable` must be real numbers.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.distinct
documentation: Return a collection of distinct items from `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.distinct_by
documentation:
Return a collection of distinct items from `iterable` using `selector` as the key.

If two items in `iterable` map to the same value from `selector`, the first one
in the iteration order of `iterable` wins.

`selector` must return a hashable value.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.drop
documentation: Drop the first `number` items from `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.drop_while
documentation: Drop the first items from `iterable` that matching `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.filter_indexed
documentation:
Filter `iterable` to items satisfying `predicate`.

`predicate` is called with both the item and its index in the original iterable.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.filter_isinstance
documentation: Filter `iterable` for instances of `type_`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.filter_not
documentation: Filter `iterable` to items that don't satisfy `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.filter_not_none
documentation: Filter `iterable` to items that are not `None`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.first
documentation:
Return the first item of `collection` matching `predicate` or raise if no item matches.

Raises:

: ValueError: If no item matches `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.first_or_none
documentation: Return the first item of `collection` matching `predicate` or `None` if no item matches.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.flat_map
documentation: Return the collection of items yielded from calling `transform` on each item of `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.flatten
documentation: Flatten `iterables` in to a single list comprising all items from all iterables.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.fold
documentation:
Accumulates value starting from `initial_value`.

Accumulation starts from `initial_value` and applies `accumulator` from left
to right across `iterable` passing the current accumulated value with each item.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.fold_indexed
documentation:
Accumulates value starting from `initial_value`.

Accumulation starts from `initial_value` and applies `accumulator` from left
to right across `iterable` passing the current accumulated value, the current index,
and the curren item at that index.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.group_by
documentation:
Groups elements of the original `iterable` by the key returned by `key_selector`.

If `value_transform` is provided, then each element as transformed by `value_transform`
is grouped by the key returned by `key_selector` as applied to the original element.
Ignore: mypy assignment
Reason: The identity function is type (T) -> T, which is a valid function type
for use where (T) -> V is expected it simply means that V == T.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.group_by_to
documentation:
Groups elements of the original `iterable` by the key returned by `key_selector`.

The groupings are added to `destination`, which is modified in-place.

If `value_transform` is provided, then each element as transformed by `value_transform`
is grouped by the key returned by `key_selector` as applied to the original element.
Ignore: mypy assignment
Reason: The identity function is type (T) -> T, which is a valid function type
for use where (T) -> V is expected it simply means that V == T.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.is_empty
documentation: Return `True` if `iterable` is empty, `False` otherwise.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.is_not_empty
documentation: Return `True` if `iterable` is not empty, `False` otherwise.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.map_indexed
documentation: Transform elements of `iterable` by applying `mapping` to each element and its index.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.map_not_none
documentation:
Transform items in `iterable` by applying `mapping` to each element.

If `mapping` returns `None` for an element, then that element is filtered out of the
result.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.map_indexed_not_none
documentation:
Transform items in `iterable` by applying `mapping` to each element and its index.

If `mapping` returns `None` for an element, then that element is filtered out of the
result.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.max_by
documentation:
Return the first element yielding the largest value of the given `selector`.

`selector` must return something that is comparable.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.max_of
documentation:
Return the maximum value resulting from applying `transform` to each item in `iterable`.

`transform` must return something that is comparable.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.min_by
documentation:
Return the first element yielding the smallest value of the given `selector`.

`selector` must return something that is comparable.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.min_of
documentation:
Return the minimum value resulting from applying `transform` to each item in `iterable`.

`transform` must return something that is comparable.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.none
documentation: Returns `True` if no item in iterable matches `predicate` and `False` otherwise.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.on_each
documentation:
Invoke `action` on each element of `iterable`.

Returns:

: The original iterable. If the input iterable is a generator, the generator will have
  been exhausted when this function returns. If the input is a generator expression then
  it will also have been exhausted. In both cases it is probably not useful to retain a
  reference to the value returned by this function.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.on_each_indexed
documentation:
Invoke `action` on each element of `iterable` and its index.

Returns:

: The original iterable. If the input iterable is a generator, the generator will have
  been exhausted when this function returns. If the input is a generator expression then
  it will also have been exhausted. In both cases it is probably not useful to retain a
  reference to the value returned by this function.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.partition
documentation:
Partition `iterable` in to two disjoint collections using `predicate`.

Returns:
: A `left` and `right` collection. Items in the `left` collection yielded a value of `True`

  : for the given function, while items in the `right` collection yielded a value of
    `False`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.reduce
documentation:
Accumulates value from left to right starting with the first element.

`accumulator` takes the current accumulated value and the next value in `iterable`.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.reduce_indexed
documentation:
Accumulates value from left to right starting with the first element.

`accumulator` takes the accumulated value so far, as well as the next element in `iterable`
and its index.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.reduce_indexed_or_none
documentation:
Accumulates value from left to right starting with the first element.

`accumulator` takes the accumulated value so far, as well as the next element in `iterable`
and its index.

This function returns `None` if `iterable` is empty rather than raising.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.reduce_or_none
documentation:
Accumulates value from left to right starting with the first element.

`accumulator` takes the accumulated value so far, as well as the next element in `iterable`.

This function returns `None` if `iterable` is empty rather than raising.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.running_fold
documentation:
Return a running list of accumulated values applying `operation` from left to right.

The resulting list starts with `initial` and `operation` is called with the previously
accumulated value and the next value in `iterable`.

For the first item in `iterable`, `operation` is called with `initial` and the first
value.

Warning:

: The accumulated value should not be mutated otherwise it can affect the preceding values.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.running_fold_indexed
documentation:
Return a running list of accumulated values applying `operation` from left to right.

The resulting list starts with `initial` and `operation` is called with the previously
accumulated value and the next value in `iterable` along with its index.

For the first item in `iterable`, `operation` is called with `initial` and the first
value.

Warning:

: The accumulated value should not be mutated otherwise it can affect the preceding values.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.single
documentation:
Return the single item in `iterable`.

If `predicate` is provided, then this returns the single item matching `predicate`.

Raises:

: StopIteration: if `iterable` is empty.
  ValueError: if `iterable` contains more than one item.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.single_or_none
documentation:
Return the single item in `iterable`.

If `predicate` is provided, then this returns the single item matching `predicate`.

This function returns `None` if `iterable` is empty or has more than one matching
value instead of raising.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.sum_of
documentation:
Accumulate a sum of each item in `iterable` given the value of `selector` for that item.

If `selector` returns an {py:obj}`int` for each value, then the return value
will be an {py:obj}`int`. Otherwise, if `selector` returns a {py:obj}`float` then
the return value will be a {py:obj}`float`.
Type hints intentionally left out of the signature since @overload is being used

functions.name: collektions._iterable.take
documentation: Yield the first `n` items of `iterable`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.take_while
documentation: Consume items from `iterable` while they continue matching `predicate`.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.unzip
documentation:
Decompose a zip'd `iterable` in to its constituent left and right-hand iterables.

This function performs the inverse operation that {py:func}`zip` does.

functions.name: collektions._iterable.windowed
documentation:
Return a sliding window view of `iterable` with window size `size`.

If the length of `iterable` is not evenly divisible by `size` and `allow_partial`
is `True`, then the remaining len(iterable) - len(iterable) // size items will be returned
in a partial window. If `allow_partial` is `False`, then only full-size windows
will be returned.

The window moves `step` steps each time.

variables.name: collektions._iterable.find
documentation: An alias for first_or_none for situations where `find` makes more sense contextually.
